ST. PAUL, Mimi, Dec. Some
how this big switchmens strike,
that has all but paralyzed the
northwest, has a strong resem-
blance TO the familiar scene down
in the old orchard, where a swarm
of busy, home loving bees, resent
ing the offices of two legged in
truder, gets busy in primitive fash
ion to show its displeasure.


These $4,000 or so switchmen
have been buzzing about the ears
of one James j. Hill. raising such a
din that others have stopped to
look ON. Yes, there is general
managers committee that has SUI
prem charge of the situation. 1t in
cludes an array of imposing names
and represents millions of dollars; ;
but as far as the switchmen of the
st. Paul district are concerned, the
railroads are Hill-cool, crafty, ex-
perienced Jim Hill. winner of a
hundred fights, absolute boss of the
northern transcontinentals. He is
the two legged embodiment of
power that the pigmy bees are be-
siesing.s


WILL HAWLEY BEST HILL.


But the swltchmen know this:
Hin has been beaten before. And
he knows when he's worsted. If
he's d desperate, resourccful
fighter, hes also good loser. Eu-
gene Dsbs taught him the trick a
good many years ago. in the early
days of;the A. r. U. Hin fought to
the Last ditch, was whipped, and
surrendered without a whlmper.
Franz T. Hawley, the switch.
mems president. hopes to repeat
Debs' Victory He is the queen
bee" and be Is onto his job. No
one, not even Hill, underestimate
him.


What are the switchmen striking
for.


Cstensibly and technically a
wage advance of six cents an hour
and improved working conditions
fundamentally THEIR LIVES!

northern regions nature must be
